title: Eli Bradley
content:
{{Character |image = Eli Bradley Headshot.png |real name = {{Conjecture|Elijah}} Bradley |alias = {{Alias |primary = Eli Bradley{{Ref|TFaTWS102}}}} |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA}} |gender = Male |tv series = ''[[The Falcon and The Winter Soldier]]'' (3 episodes) |actor = [[Elijah Richardson]] |status = Alive}} {{Quote|You must not hear what I just said. You ain't getting in this [[Bradley Residence|house]]. Y'all can leave now.|Eli Bradley to [[Bucky Barnes]]|The Star-Spangled Man}} '''{{Conjecture|Elijah}} "Eli" Bradley''' is the grandson of [[Isaiah Bradley]]. ==Biography== ===Hero's Grandson=== ====Isaiah Bradley's Visitors==== {{Quote|Today's your lucky day. [[Isaiah Bradley|He]] said he wanna see for himself.|Eli Bradley to [[Bucky Barnes]]|The Star-Spangled Man}} [[File:Isaiah Bradley's House.png|{{AltFloat}}|Bradley sees [[Bucky Barnes]] and [[Sam Wilson]]]] In [[2024]], Eli Bradley was at his grandfather [[Isaiah Bradley]]'s house in [[Baltimore]], [[Maryland]], when [[Sam Wilson]] and [[Bucky Barnes]] arrived to meet his grandfather. Initially, Bradley did not let them inside; however, he did after Barnes revealed he knew Isaiah. Bradley stood in the back of the room quietly during the conversation between his grandfather, Barnes, and Wilson. After his grandfather demanded the two leave, Bradley let them out.{{Ref|TFaTWS102}} ====Sam Wilson's Return==== [[File:Eli Bradley & Sam Wilson.png|{{AltFloat}}|Bradley speaks privately with [[Sam Wilson]]]] One evening, Bradley was out playing basketball with a friend when he saw [[Sam Wilson]] walking down the street towards his [[Isaiah Bradley|grandfather]]'s house. He recognized him from meeting him a few days ago and asked him what he was doing there, before telling him that he would be back home shortly.{{Ref|TFaTWS105}} ====Legacy of Isaiah Bradley==== [[File:Eli and Isaiah watch Falcon.png|{{AltFloat}}|Bradley watches [[Sam Wilson]] on the news]] {{Quote|What you want, Black Falcon?"
"Hey, you need to learn some manners.|Eli Bradley and [[Sam Wilson]]|One World, One People}} A few days later, Bradley and his [[Isaiah Bradley|grandfather]] were eating dinner, but stopped upon seeing [[Sam Wilson]], in his new [[Captain America's Uniform|Captain America Suit]], on the [[WHiH World News]]. They listened as Wilson gave a speech to [[GRC]] officials about having taken up the Captain America mantle and what that meant to him. [[File:Sam Wilson, Isaiah Bradley & Eli Bradley.png|{{AltFloat}}|Bradley demands to go with his [[Isaiah Bradley|grandfather]]]] The next day, Bradley answered the door and saw Wilson standing outside. Upon seeing Wilson, Bradley teasingly called him the "Black Falcon", to which Wilson told him he needed to have manners. Bradley then came outside later as Wilson and his grandfather talked on the front porch. When hearing that they were going somewhere, Bradley got excited and asked when and where they were going, to Wilson's surprise thinking that he would not be interested. [[File:Eli Bradley, Isaiah Bradley & Sam Wilson.png|{{AltFloat}}|Bradley visiting the [[Smithsonian Institution]]]] Bradley then went with Wilson and his grandfather to the [[Smithsonian Institution]] in [[Washington, D.C.]]. They walked through the Captain America Exhibit and to the other side of it, where Wilson showed them a statue of Isaiah and a plaque on his history. Bradley watched as Isaiah, who was emotional, hugged Wilson and thanked him for the memorial.{{Ref|TFaTWS106}} {{Clear}} ==Facilities== *'''[[Bradley Residence]]''': Eli lived with his grandfather [[Isaiah Bradley]] in his grandfather's house in [[Baltimore]], [[Maryland]] and took care of him after the death of his grandmother [[Faith Bradley]]. ==Relationships== ===Family=== *[[Isaiah Bradley]] - Grandfather *[[Faith Bradley]] † - Grandmother ===Allies=== *[[Bucky Barnes]] *[[Captain America|Sam Wilson/Falcon/Captain America]] ==Appearances== In chronological order: *{{Appearance|TFaTWS102}} *{{Appearance|TFaTWS105}} *{{Appearance|TFaTWS106}} ==Trivia== *In the comics, Elijah Bradley became the superhero '''[[Patriot]]''', a member of the [[w:c:marvel:Young Avengers|Young Avengers]]. Although he claimed that he inherited his super-strength from his grandfather [[Isaiah Bradley|Isaiah]], his powers actually came from his use of [[w:c:marvel:Mutant Growth Hormone|Mutant Growth Hormone]]. Eventually, he obtained permanent superpowers after receiving a blood transfusion from his grandfather. ==Behind the Scenes== *[[Ryan Coogler]] and [[Joe Robert Cole]] considered the idea of introducing Elijah Bradley in an early draft of ''[[Black Panther (film)|Black Panther]]''.[https://screenrant.com/black-panther-patriot-young-avengers/ Black Panther Almost Included Patriot, Says Ryan Coogler] *According to [[Nate Moore]], Eli Bradley was featured in early drafts of ''[[Captain America: Brave New World]]''. His appearance was eventually scrapped due to the film having too many characters.[https://screenrant.com/captain-america-brave-new-world-eli-bradley-cut-explained-nate-moore/ Captain America: Brave New World Cut A Falcon And The Winter Soldier Character With The Potential To Join The MCU's Young Avengers, Marvel Producer Explains Why] ==References== {{Gallery|Eli Bradley/Gallery}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel|Elijah Bradley}} *{{WP|Patriot (comics)#Patriot (Eli Bradley)|Elijah Bradley}} [[pt-br:Eli Bradley]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:The Falcon and The Winter Soldier Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Black Hair]] [[Category:Brown Eyes]]
